summary: eth-wallet-sentinel exfiltrates host/system data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package contains an install-time exfiltration payload in package.json (line 30) and a sibling scanner.js (~270+ lines) that together collect host identifiers and POST them to a hardcoded attacker endpoint. The package.json embeds an inline Node command that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers hostname/cwd/environment data, and POSTs to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js extends the same pattern: it requires fs/os/https/child_process, calls os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), enumerates filesystem paths via fs.existsSync, issues https.request/https.get POSTs, and additionally invokes curl, all targeting the same https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint. The destination is a personal GitHub Pages site unrelated to any plausible Ethereum wallet sentinel publisher and is used as a beacon/C2 receiver. Installing or loading this package transmits installer host identity and system info to an attacker-controlled host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
